name: CONTRACT-BUG-46
title: test-templates-prompts-bundle.py asserts template_version 1.0.0 for tdd-subagent.txt but template was bumped to 1.1.0 in 0fc995cb
description:
## Repro

`python3 .claude/features/contract/test/run.py` exits nonzero. The failing test is `test-templates-prompts-bundle.py`:

- The test (line 57) defines `MARKER = "# template_version: 1.0.0"` and applies that single hardcoded marker to ALL eight bundled templates under `.claude/features/contract/templates/prompts/`.
- `templates/prompts/tdd-subagent.txt` was bumped from `1.0.0` to `1.1.0` in commit 0fc995cb (`fix(tdd-subagent): template emits test_result placeholder + nonzero-exit fail-HANDOFF`) — a legitimate minor-version bump for new template behavior.
- That commit updated the template but did NOT update the contract test's hardcoded marker, nor Inv 57's wording (which also says `# template_version: 1.0.0` as if all eight files share one version).

## Impact

Any TDD cycle on contract gets blocked at TEST-GREEN by this pre-existing drift, even when the cycle's own work is correct. Discovered during RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-106 cycle; that cycle's impl is correct but cannot complete until this drift is reconciled.

## Suggested fix (one-line test update + optional spec wording tweak)

Option A (simplest): change `test-templates-prompts-bundle.py` so MARKER assertion is per-file (e.g., a dict mapping filename to expected version) OR a regex like `^# template_version: \d+\.\d+\.\d+$` that accepts any semver.

Option B (preserves single MARKER constant): keep `MARKER = "# template_version: 1.0.0"` for the seven skill passthroughs but special-case tdd-subagent.txt to `1.1.0`.

Option A is more future-proof — any future template bump won't recreate this drift. Inv 57 text should also be updated to drop the literal `1.0.0` and say "a canonical plain-text `# template_version: X.Y.Z` marker (semver)".

## Why this is a contract bug and not a tdd-subagent bug

The version bump on tdd-subagent.txt was correct. The test in contract is the broken artifact — it conflates 'all eight files MUST share the same version' with 'all eight files MUST carry the canonical marker shape'. The Inv 57 text shares the same conflation.

## Suggested test gap

A meta-test on the test-templates-prompts-bundle.py assertion: if any future template bump produces a per-file version, the test still passes (regression test for this exact class of drift).

history.note: Inline fix landed in commit 6cf4f724 (per-file semver-shape MARKER_RE in test-templates-prompts-bundle.py; passthroughs pinned at 1.0.0; Inv 57 wording loosened). Filed and closed in the same session to unblock RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-106 cycle.
